channel.title: Plate Discipline
channel.description: Data-driven visual essays about baseball. Spin rates, swing decisions, and probability shifts, designed to be felt, not just read.

item.title: The Slider Epidemic
item.description: Slider usage surged from 15% in 2015 to 25% in 2025, making it baseball's most dominant pitch — and a leading driver of elbow injuries.

item.title: The Death of the Fastball
item.description: Average fastball usage dropped from 65% in 2008 to 49% in 2025. Hitters got too good at timing velocity, so pitching evolved away from its own signature weapon.

item.title: The Cutter Took Over Baseball
item.description: Cutter usage tripled from 4.2% to 12.8% of all pitches between 2018 and 2025. It is now the third most common pitch in baseball.

item.title: What 100 MPH Used to Mean
item.description: How triple-digit fastballs evolved from 0.4% of pitches in 2010 to 3.8% in 2025, transforming what elite velocity means in baseball.

item.title: The Dead Ball Is Back
item.description: League-wide home run rates dropped 12% between 2023 and 2025. The ball changed. Again.

item.title: The Closer Is a Myth
item.description: Save percentage tells you almost nothing about reliever quality. The best reliever on most teams doesn't pitch the 9th inning. The closer role costs teams wins.

item.title: Pitch Clock and the Rhythm of the Game
item.description: The clock cut 26 minutes off an average game. It also changed strikeout rates, walk rates, stolen base attempts, and how pitchers sequence their arsenals.

item.title: How Spring Training Stats Lie
item.description: The correlation between a hitter's spring training batting average and his regular season batting average is 0.07. That is almost zero.
